member.name: M:libplctag.DataTypes.Extensions.ArrayExtensions.To1DArray``1(``0[0:,0:])
member.summary: Extension method to flatten a 2D array to a 1D array

member.name: M:libplctag.DataTypes.Extensions.ArrayExtensions.To1DArray``1(``0[0:,0:,0:])
member.summary: Extension method to flatten a 3D array to a 1D array

member.name: M:libplctag.DataTypes.Extensions.ArrayExtensions.To2DArray``1(``0[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Extension method to reshape a 1D array into a 2D array

member.name: M:libplctag.DataTypes.Extensions.ArrayExtensions.To3DArray``1(``0[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Extension method to reshape a 1D array into a 3D array

member.name: P:libplctag.DataTypes.IPlcMapper`1.PlcType
member.summary: You can define different marshalling behaviour for different types The PlcType is injected during PlcMapper instantiation, and will be available to you in your marshalling logic

member.name: P:libplctag.DataTypes.IPlcMapper`1.ElementSize
member.summary: Provide an integer value for ElementSize if you want to pass this into the tag constructor

member.name: P:libplctag.DataTypes.IPlcMapper`1.ArrayDimensions
member.summary: The dimensions of the array. Null if not an array.

member.name: M:libplctag.DataTypes.IPlcMapper`1.GetElementCount
member.summary: This is used to convert the number of array elements into the raw element count, which is used by the library. Most of the time, this will be the dimensions multiplied, but occasionally it is not (e.g. BOOL arrays).

member.name: M:libplctag.DataTypes.IPlcMapper`1.Decode(libplctag.Tag)
member.summary: This is the method that reads/unpacks the underlying value of the tag and returns it as a C# type

member.name: M:libplctag.DataTypes.IPlcMapper`1.Encode(libplctag.Tag,`0)
member.summary: This is the method that transforms the C# type into the underlying value of the tag

member.name: T:libplctag.DebugLevel
member.summary: Debug levels available in the base libplctag library

member.name: T:libplctag.Event
member.summary: Events returned by the base libplctag library

member.name: T:libplctag.ITag
member.summary: An interface to represent any generic tag without exposing its value

member.name: T:libplctag.LibPlcTag
member.summary: A static class used to access some additional features of the libplctag base library

member.name: M:libplctag.LibPlcTag.IsRequiredVersion(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Check if base library meets version requirements

member.name: P:libplctag.LibPlcTag.DebugLevel
member.summary: Sets a debug level for the underlying libplctag library

member.name: E:libplctag.LibPlcTag.LogEvent
member.summary: You can redirect all logging from the library to your own delegate.
member.remarks: WARNING There are some important restrictions on logging delegates: The delegate will be called from multiple threads, sometimes simultaneously! Your code must be thread aware and thread-safe. The delegate will be called with one or more mutexes held in almost all cases.You must not call any tag API functions other than plc_tag_decode_error(). If you do there is a large chance that the library will hang. Logging messages come from deep within the library's core routines. Many of these are very delay sensitive. Do not do anything that would block or delay the return of the logging callback to the library! The message string passed to your callback function will be managed by the library. Do not attempt to free its memory or modify the string. If you need to do modifications, make your own copy and return.

member.name: M:libplctag.LibPlcTag.Shutdown
member.summary: After this function returns, the library will have cleaned up all internal threads and resources. You can immediately turn around and call plc_tag_create() again and the library will start up again. Note: you must dispose of all Tags before calling
member.remarks: Some wrappers and systems are not able to trigger the standard POSIX or Windows functions when the library is being unloaded or the program is shutting down. In those cases, you can call this function.

member.name: T:libplctag.LibPlcTagException
member.summary: An exception thrown by the underlying libplctag library

member.name: F:libplctag.PlcType.ControlLogix
member.summary: Control Logix-class PLC. Synonym for lgx, logix, controllogix, contrologix, compactlogix, clgx.

member.name: F:libplctag.PlcType.Plc5
member.summary: PLC/5 PLC. Synonym for plc5, plc.

member.name: F:libplctag.PlcType.Slc500
member.summary: SLC 500 PLC. Synonym for slc500, slc.

member.name: F:libplctag.PlcType.LogixPccc
member.summary: Control Logix-class PLC using the PLC/5 protocol. Synonym for lgxpccc, logixpccc, lgxplc5, lgx_pccc, logix_pccc, lgx_plc5.

member.name: F:libplctag.PlcType.Micro800
member.summary: Micro800-class PLC. Synonym for micrologix800, mlgx800, micro800.

member.name: F:libplctag.PlcType.MicroLogix
member.summary: MicroLogix PLC. Synonym for micrologix, mlgx.

member.name: F:libplctag.PlcType.Omron
member.summary: Omron PLC. Synonym for omron-njnx, omron-nj, omron-nx, njnx, nx1p2

member.name: F:libplctag.Protocol.ab_eip
member.summary: Allen-Bradley specific flavor of EIP

member.name: F:libplctag.Protocol.modbus_tcp
member.summary: A Modbus TCP implementation used by many PLCs

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.IsInitialized
member.summary: True if or has been called.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.ElementCount
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] An integer number of elements per tag.
member.remarks: All tags are treated as arrays. Tags that are not arrays are considered to have a length of one element. This attribute determines how many elements are in the tag. Defaults to one (1) if not found.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.ElementSize
member.summary: [REQUIRED/OPTIONAL] An integer number of bytes per element
member.remarks: This attribute determines the size of a single element of the tag. Ignored for Modbus and for Allen-Bradley PLCs.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.Gateway
member.summary: [REQUIRED] IP address or host name

remarks.para: This tells the library what host name or IP address to use for the PLC or the gateway to the PLC (in the case that the PLC is remote).

[AB-SPECIFIC] Only for PLCs with additional routing. This attribute is required for CompactLogix/ControlLogix tags and for tags using a DH+ protocol bridge (i.e. a DHRIO module) to get to a PLC/5, SLC 500, or MicroLogix PLC on a remote DH+ link. The attribute is ignored if it is not a DH+ bridge route, but will generate a warning if debugging is active. Note that Micro800 connections must not have a path attribute.

listheader.description:
The full name of the tag, e.g. `TestBigTag[10,42]` for CIP-based PLCs.
For program tags, prepend `Program:{program name}.` where {program name} is the name of the program in which the tag is created. Example: `Program:MyProgram.MyTag`. This will access the tag MyTag in the program MyProgram. For PCCC-based PLCs, examples include `N7:4`, `ST18:26`, `L20:2`, `B3:4/2`, `MG14:6.en` etc. Many common field name abbeviations are supported.

The type and first register number of a tag, e.g. co42 for coil 42 (counts from zero).
The supported register type prefixes are co for coil, di for discrete inputs, hr for holding registers and ir for input registers. The type prefix must be present and the register number must be greater than or equal to zero and less than or equal to 65535. Modbus examples: co21 - coil 21, di22 - discrete input 22, hr66 - holding register 66, ir64000 - input register 64000.

Only for PLCs with additional routing.
This attribute is required for CompactLogix/ControlLogix tags and for tags using a DH+ protocol bridge (i.e. a DHRIO module) to get to a PLC/5, SLC 500, or MicroLogix PLC on a remote DH+ link. The attribute is ignored if it is not a DH+ bridge route, but will generate a warning if debugging is active. Note that Micro800 connections must not have a path attribute.

The server/unit ID. Must be an integer value between 0 and 255.
Servers may support more than one unit or may bridge to other units. Example: path=4 for accessing device unit ID 4.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.PlcType
member.summary: [REQUIRED | AB-SPECIFIC] Determines the type of the PLC.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.Protocol
member.summary: [REQUIRED] Determines the type of the PLC Protocol.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.ReadCacheMillisecondDuration
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] Use this attribute to cause the tag read operations to cache data the requested number of milliseconds.
member.remarks: This can be used to lower the actual number of requests against the PLC. Example read_cache_ms=100 will result in read operations no more often than once every 100 milliseconds.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.Timeout
member.summary: A timeout value that is used for Initialize/Read/Write methods. It applies to both synchronous and asynchronous calls.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.UseConnectedMessaging
member.summary: [OPTIONAL | AB-SPECIFIC] True = use CIP connection. False = use UCMM.
member.remarks: Control whether to use connected or unconnected messaging. Only valid on Logix-class PLCs. Connected messaging is required on Micro800 and DH+ bridged links. Default is PLC-specific and link-type specific. Generally you do not need to set this.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.AllowPacking
member.summary: [OPTIONAL | AB-SPECIFIC] True = (default) allow use of multi-request CIP command. False = use only one CIP request per packet.
member.remarks: This is specific to individual PLC models. Generally only Control Logix-class PLCs support it. It is the default for those PLCs that support it as it greatly increases the performance of the communication channel to the PLC.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.AutoSyncReadInterval
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] An integer number of milliseconds to periodically read data from the PLC.
member.remarks: Use this attribute to automatically read data from the PLC on a set interval. This can be used in conjunction with the and events to respond to the data updates.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.AutoSyncWriteInterval
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] An integer number of milliseconds to buffer tag data changes before writing to the PLC.
member.remarks: Use this attribute to automatically write data to the PLC a set duration after setting its value. This can be used to lower the actual number of write operations by locally buffering local writes, and only writing to the PLC the most recent one when the wait completes. You can determine when a write starts and completes by catching the and events.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.Int16ByteOrder
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] A string indicating the byte order of 16-bit integers.
member.remarks: Allowable values include `01` (little-endian) and `10` (big-endian). Defaults to `10` for Modbus and `01` for Allen-Bradley.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.Int32ByteOrder
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] A string indicating the byte order of 32-bit integers.
member.remarks: Defaults to `3210`, strict big-endian, for Modbus and `0123` for Allen-Bradley PLCs.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.Int64ByteOrder
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] A string indicating the byte order of 64-bit integers.
member.remarks: Defaults to `76543210`, strict big-endian, for Modbus and `01234567` for Allen-Bradley PLCs.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.Float32ByteOrder
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] A string indicating the byte order of 32-bit floating point values.
member.remarks: Defaults to `3210`, strict big-endian, for Modbus. Allen-Bradley PLCs default to the PLC-native order.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.Float64ByteOrder
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] A string indicating the byte order of 64-bit floating point values.
member.remarks: Defaults to `76543210`, strict big-endian, for Modbus and `01234567` for Allen-Bradley PLCs.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.StringCountWordBytes
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] A positive integer value of 1, 2, 4, or 8 determining how big the leading count word is in a string.
member.remarks: Defaults are set per PLC type. AB Logix PLCs default to 4. AB PCCC PLCs default to 2. Must be used with .

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.StringIsByteSwapped
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] Determines whether character bytes are swapped within 16-bit words.
member.remarks: Defaults are set per PLC type. AB Logix PLCs default to false and PCCC PLCs default to true.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.StringIsCounted
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] Determines whether strings have a count word or not.
member.remarks: Defaults are set per PLC type. AB PLCs default to true. If set true, must be used with .

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.StringIsFixedLength
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] Determines whether strings have a fixed length that they occupy.
member.remarks: Defaults are set per PLC and tag type. AB defaults to true for ControlLogix and CompactLogix and 84 for PCCC-based PLCs. Listing tags is an exception as the tag names are counted, but not fixed length.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.StringIsZeroTerminated
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] Determines whether strings are zero-terminated as is done in C.
member.remarks: Defaults are set per PLC type. AB defaults to false.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.StringMaxCapacity
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] Determines the maximum number of character bytes in a string.
member.remarks: Defaults are set per PLC type. AB Logix and PCCC PLCs default to 82.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.StringPadBytes
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] A positive integer value determining the total number of padding bytes at the end of a string.
member.remarks: Defaults are set per PLC type. AB Logix PLCs default to 2. AB PCCC PLCs default to 0.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.StringTotalLength
member.summary: [OPTIONAL] A positive integer value determining the total number of bytes used in the tag buffer by a string. Must be used with .
member.remarks: Defaults are set per PLC type. AB Logix PLCs default to 88. AB PCCC PLCs default to 84.

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag.MaxRequestsInFlight
member.summary: [OPTIONAL | MODBUS-SPECIFIC] The Modbus specification allows devices to queue up to 16 requests at once.
member.remarks: The default is 1 and the maximum is 16. This allows the host to send multiple requests without waiting for the device to respond. Not all devices support up to 16 requests in flight.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.Initialize
member.summary: Creates the underlying data structures and references required before tag operations.
member.remarks: Initializes the tag by establishing necessary connections. Can only be called once per instance. Timeout is controlled via class property.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.InitializeAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates the underlying data structures and references required before tag operations.
member.remarks: Initializes the tag by establishing necessary connections. Can only be called once per instance. Timeout is controlled via class property.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.Read
member.summary: Executes a synchronous read on a tag. Timeout is controlled via class property.
member.remarks: Reading a tag brings the data at the time of read into the local memory of the PC running the library. The data is not automatically kept up to date. If you need to find out the data periodically, you need to read the tag periodically.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.ReadAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes an asynchronous read on a tag. Timeout is controlled via class property.
member.remarks: Reading a tag brings the data at the time of read into the local memory of the PC running the library. The data is not automatically kept up to date. If you need to find out the data periodically, you need to read the tag periodically.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.Write
member.summary: Executes a synchronous write on a tag. Timeout is controlled via class property.
member.remarks: Writing a tag sends the data from local memory to the target PLC.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.WriteAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes an asynchronous write on a tag. Timeout is controlled via class property.
member.remarks: Writing a tag sends the data from local memory to the target PLC.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.GetBuffer
member.summary: This function retrieves a segment of raw, unprocessed bytes from the tag buffer.
member.remarks: Note; allocates a new block of memory. If this is problematic, use instead.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.GetBuffer(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Fills the supplied buffer with the raw, unprocessed bytes from the tag buffer.
member.remarks: Use this instead of to avoid creating a new block of memory.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.GetBuffer(System.Int32,System.Byte[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Fills the supplied buffer with the raw, unprocessed bytes from the tag buffer.
member.remarks: Use this instead of to avoid creating a new block of memory.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.GetByteArrayAttribute(System.String)
member.summary: This function retrieves an attribute of the raw tag byte array.

member.name: M:libplctag.Tag.GetStatus
member.summary: Check the operational status of the tag

member.name: T:libplctag.Tag`2
member.summary: A class that allows for strongly-typed objects tied to PLC tags
member.typeparam: A class that handles data conversion
The desired C# type of Tag.Value

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag`2.ArrayDimensions
member.summary: Dimensions of Value if it is an array Ex. {2, 10} for a 2 column, 10 row array Non-arrays can use null (default)

member.name: P:libplctag.Tag`2.Value
member.summary: The local memory value that can be transferred to/from the PLC
